pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 9, export_result.sh line 8, verifier.py line 51
Metadata field exists but is NOT used in verifier.py - no minimum file size check is performed. export_result.sh captures file_size_bytes but verifier.py never validates against this threshold.
Code analysis: verifier.py line 102 checks 'if arr.shape[0] < 400' - the actual minimum width enforced is 400 pixels, not 800
Code analysis: verifier.py line 102 checks 'if arr.shape[1] < 400' - the actual minimum height enforced is 400 pixels, not 600

Code analysis: setup_invesalius.sh lines 70-73 show CRANIUM_DIR="$SAMPLE_ROOT/ct_cranium" and CRANIUM_URL="https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip". The 0051.zip file is downloaded and extracted to ct_cranium directory. GitHub URL redirect confirmed the asset exists.

privileged_info_summary: Task uses real medical imaging data: CT Cranium dataset (case 0051) from official InVesalius GitHub v3.0 releases (https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip). Expected output is a PNG pseudocolor rendering exported to /home/ga/Documents/pseudocolor_view.png. Verification discrepancies: (1) Metadata claims minimum dimensions of 800x600 pixels, but verifier only enforces 400x400 minimum; (2) Metadata specifies 50KB minimum file size in uncommon 'min_file_size_kb' format, but this is never validated in verifier.py (unlike other tasks which use 'min_file_size_bytes'). The dataset itself is real, publicly available medical imaging data.
